site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 19 preview after the Origin decider
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 19 tips, post-Origin late mail, and Mark Hughes Foundation ledger tracking.

featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 19 is a seven-game slate played immediately after the State of Origin decider.
The cleanest raw edge is Storm over Titans because Jahrome Hughes, Nick Meaney and Jack Howarth are all on track to return, while Munster, Grant and Loiero are named to back up.
Sharks over Dolphins remains strong because Jack Bostock is set to miss and several Dolphins Origin players are still being monitored.
Warriors over Wests Tigers is a good Friday lean despite Barnett and Capewell dropping out, because James Fisher-Harris and Leka Halasima are on track to return while the Tigers lose Api Koroisau.

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: This is a post-Origin round, so final 24-hour cuts matter more than the first named teams.
James Tedesco is set to miss for the Roosters, which pushes the Eels lean up, but Mitchell Moses still has to back up cleanly from Origin.
Bulldogs v Raiders is the most fragile pick because Crichton, Young and Strange all sit in the Origin back-up watch zone.
Sea Eagles v Cowboys can flip if North Queensland's Origin trio all back up strongly and Tom Dearden comes in late.

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the positive Mark Hughes Foundation ledger at +$0.53.
Keep Round 19 as a tips board until a price, stake and charity-bet confirmation are recorded.
Treat Storm as the first price-watch candidate, then Sharks if the market is sensible.
Do not turn late-mail uncertainty into a bigger stake.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Check Friday 24-hour cuts for Tigers v Warriors, especially Warriors forward reshuffle after Barnett and Capewell drop out.
Monitor Dolphins and Sharks reserves: Bostock is out, Nikora likely rests, Brailey expected to back up and Fonua-Blake is a game-day call.
Watch Roosters fullback replacement for Tedesco and whether Sam Walker or Reece Robson comes into the side.
Confirm Ponga, Bradman Best, Cameron Murray, Munster, Grant and Loiero all remain in their final teams.

betCard.title: Round 19 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Friday 10 July to Sunday 12 July 2026
why: Storm over Titans is the best raw tip, but no TAB price and stake have been recorded.
Sharks over Dolphins is also a watch candidate, but both sides still have post-Origin availability questions.
A charity bet only becomes official after explicit user confirmation before kickoff.
risks: Origin back-up decisions can change team strength quickly across Saturday and Sunday.
A short turnaround after the Origin decider makes fatigue and late withdrawals a real risk.
Any Round 19 charity play still needs recorded odds, stake, match, and explicit confirmation before kickoff.

watchlist: Storm over Titans is the top confidence tip, but only a price-watch candidate until odds and stake are recorded.
Sharks over Dolphins is strong but still needs final calls on Fonua-Blake, Brailey, Nikora and the Dolphins Origin group.
Warriors over Wests Tigers is a Friday lean even with Barnett and Capewell out because the Tigers lose Api Koroisau.
Bulldogs over Raiders is low-confidence because both sides have important Origin and injury watch points.
Eels over Roosters leans on Tedesco's expected absence and Moses backing up.
confidence: Storm over Titans - best raw edge with Hughes, Meaney and Howarth returning and Grant, Munster and Loiero named
Sharks over Dolphins - Dolphins lose Bostock and still have Origin availability calls across their reserves
Warriors over Wests Tigers - Tigers lose Api Koroisau while Warriors regain James Fisher-Harris and Leka Halasima
Knights over Rabbitohs - Ponga and Bradman Best are expected to back up while Souths lose Cody Walker
Sea Eagles over Cowboys - Manly home lean with Tom Trbojevic, Fogarty and Haumole Olakau'atu
Bulldogs over Raiders - home lean only, with Crichton backing up and Raiders still juggling Origin/injury returns
Eels over Roosters - upset lean with Tedesco set to miss and Moses expected to back up

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 19 starts one day after the Origin decider
lines: As of Thursday, 9 July 2026, Round 19 late mail is live.
The round runs from Friday 10 July to Sunday 12 July 2026.
The seven tips are Warriors, Sharks, Bulldogs, Eels, Knights, Sea Eagles and Storm.
The charity ledger remains 3-2 with +$0.53 settled profit because no Round 19 charity bet has been locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Storm are the watch candidate, not a locked charity play
lines: Storm over Titans is the clearest card lean, but it is still a price-watch item until a fresh TAB number is recorded.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
No Round 19 charity result should be added without a pre-recorded stake, price and explicit charity-bet confirmation.
Post-Origin late withdrawals are the main reason to keep staking discipline tight this week.

sections.title: Round 19 Tips
sections.heading: Confidence order for the post-Origin round
lines: Storm over Titans - top pick; Melbourne regain Jahrome Hughes, Nick Meaney and Jack Howarth, with Munster, Grant and Loiero named to back up.
Sharks over Dolphins - Cronulla keep Hynes and Trindall together, while Bostock is set to miss and Dolphins' Origin group is still monitored.
Warriors over Wests Tigers - Warriors regain James Fisher-Harris and Leka Halasima; Tigers regain Luai but lose Api Koroisau.
Knights over Rabbitohs - Newcastle get the Ponga/Best/Dylan Brown spine-and-strike edge, while Souths lose Cody Walker to suspension.
Sea Eagles over Cowboys - 4 Pines lean with Tom Trbojevic, Jamal Fogarty, Koula and Haumole Olakau'atu named.
Bulldogs over Raiders - home lean only; Crichton is expected to back up and Kiraz is named despite a rib issue.
Eels over Roosters - upset lean because Tedesco is set to miss and the Roosters are reshuffling at fullback.

sections.title: Round 19 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 19 fixture board
lines: Wests Tigers v Warriors - Friday 10 July, 8:00pm at Campbelltown Stadium.
Dolphins v Sharks - Saturday 11 July, 3:00pm at Kayo Stadium.
Bulldogs v Raiders - Saturday 11 July, 5:30pm at Accor Stadium.
Roosters v Eels - Saturday 11 July, 7:35pm at Allianz Stadium.
Rabbitohs v Knights - Sunday 12 July, 2:00pm at Accor Stadium.
Sea Eagles v Cowboys - Sunday 12 July, 4:05pm at 4 Pines Park.
Storm v Titans - Sunday 12 July, 6:15pm at AAMI Park.
Byes: Broncos, Panthers and Dragons.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: What the week asks us to respect
lines: Do not over-weight Tuesday's team lists when Thursday late mail has already moved major players.
Tedesco and Bostock head knocks directly change Roosters/Eels and Dolphins/Sharks confidence.
Barnett and Capewell dropping out weakens the Warriors, but the Tigers losing Koroisau keeps the Warriors lean alive.
Bulldogs v Raiders and Sea Eagles v Cowboys are the two most likely games to shift after final cut-downs.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 19 late-mail details driving the card
lines: Wests Tigers regain Jarome Luai and Samuela Fainu but lose Api Koroisau; Warriors lose Mitchell Barnett and Kurt Capewell but regain James Fisher-Harris, Leka Halasima and Alofiana Khan-Pereira.
Dolphins winger Jack Bostock is set to miss after an Origin head knock; Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Selwyn Cobbo, Max Plath and Thomas Flegler remain back-up decisions.
Sharks likely rest Briton Nikora, expect Blayke Brailey to back up, and will decide on Addin Fonua-Blake closer to game day.
Roosters need to replace James Tedesco after his Origin head knock, while Parramatta expect Mitchell Moses to back up.
Storm regain Jahrome Hughes, Nick Meaney and Jack Howarth; Titans expect Tino Fa'asuamaleaui and Jojo Fifita to back up.
